key_findings: Five-lane pipeline architecture (DNS → HTTP → Enrich → Ingest → Validate) provides clean isolation and independent failure domains
Dedup key contracts are the single highest-risk contract surface; must be locked before first production write
Write-ahead checkpointing + MERGE semantics + ReplacingMergeTree achieves full idempotency across both datastores
Day-1 concurrency envelopes (DNS:250, HTTP:75, Enrich:25, Ingest:15) are conservative but provably stable; throughput is tunable post-validation
Token-bucket backpressure prevents Controller ingest saturation from Oracle scan outpacing
KRIL-weighted queue prioritization ensures highest-value intelligence surfaces before full pipeline completion
Oracle node is a single point of failure; checkpoint replayability mitigates data loss risk but not throughput continuity

decisions.title: Queue-per-lane partitioning
decisions.summary: Five independent AWSEM queue partitions, one per lane, each with DLQ
decisions.rationale: Decouples lanes with different throughput profiles; enables independent concurrency tuning

decisions.title: Dedup key contracts
decisions.summary: SHA-256 composite keys for DNS, HTTP, graph nodes, graph edges; ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree composite sort key

decisions.title: Idempotency via MERGE + ReplacingMergeTree
decisions.summary: Neo4j MERGE ON CREATE/MATCH; ClickHouse RMT with Redis Bloom filter for real-time pre-write dedup
decisions.rationale: Guarantees safe replay at any lane boundary; eliminates destructive overwrite risk

decisions.title: Write-ahead batch checkpoints
decisions.summary: Checkpoint written before datastore write, per 5k/2k/500-item batch by lane
decisions.rationale: Ensures deterministic replay from last confirmed state; achieves ≤10 min RTO

decisions.title: Conservative Day-1 concurrency envelopes
decisions.summary: DNS:250, HTTP:75, Enrich:25, Ingest:15 Neo4j tx, ClickHouse batch every 10s or 5k rows
decisions.rationale: Stability-first; benchmark under real load before expansion

decisions.title: Token-bucket backpressure with high/low water marks
decisions.summary: Controller suspends Oracle task issuance when result queue exceeds high-water mark; resumes at 50% low-water mark
decisions.rationale: Prevents Controller ingest saturation; decouples scan throughput from ingest capacity

decisions.title: Lane sequencing contract
decisions.summary: Lane N+1 activates for a batch only after Lane N checkpoint is confirmed
decisions.rationale: Eliminates DNS/HTTP linking race conditions; enforces graph integrity at edge creation time

decisions.title: ORS reflex monitor integration
decisions.summary: Five telemetry signals per lane with defined warning/critical thresholds and automated reflexes
decisions.rationale: Reduces operator response time; enables autonomous stability management within defined envelopes

decisions.title: KRIL-weighted queue prioritization
decisions.summary: Subdomains sorted by KRIL rank within DNS batches; HTTP/Enrich queues filter by KRIL threshold
decisions.rationale: Maximizes intelligence yield per unit of pipeline time; high-value findings surface early

decisions.title: Model escalation rate cap at 5%
decisions.summary: ORS arbitrates premium model use; escalation only when anomaly score >0.75 AND confidence <0.6
decisions.rationale: Cost discipline; prevents runaway escalation under high-noise scan conditions

risk_model.title: Oracle SPOF
risk_model.mitigation: ORS heartbeat monitoring, checkpoint replayability, future second worker

risk_model.title: ClickHouse dedup lag
risk_model.mitigation: FINAL keyword in validation queries, Redis Bloom filter pre-insertion

risk_model.title: Neo4j memory pressure at scale
risk_model.mitigation: Pre-configure heap >= 8GB, page cache >= 16GB, batched MERGE writes of 500 ops/tx

risk_model.title: DNS/HTTP linking race condition
risk_model.mitigation: Lane sequencing contract enforced at AWSEM batch issuance

risk_model.title: Dedup key false dedup via collision
risk_model.mitigation: SHA-256 keys, test corpus validation, collision audit in validation lane

risk_model.title: Controller ingest saturation
risk_model.mitigation: Token-bucket backpressure, ClickHouse bulk insert, Neo4j batch size tuning

recommended_actions.action: Lock and commit dedup key contracts to protocols/DEDUP_KEY_CONTRACT.md before first write
Implement and test write-ahead checkpoint logic with synthetic replay test before production activation
Apply Neo4j schema constraints and indexes: CREATE CONSTRAINT subdomain_name_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:Subdomain) REQUIRE n.name IS UNIQUE
Run DNS lane only for first 24–48 hours; gate HTTP lane activation on validation pass (error <=2%, dupe <=0.5%, link correctness >=99.5%)
Activate and validate ORS reflex monitors before enabling Oracle scan execution at production concurrency
Deploy Redis Bloom filter on Controller; warm with existing known subdomains pre-ingest
Post Day-1 stabilization: benchmark concurrency envelopes; step DNS from 250 to 350 to 500 with ORS monitoring
Implement ClickHouse materialized views for real-time ORS health dashboard metrics
Define AWSEM per-lane SLA contracts with maximum batch processing time thresholds

implementation_phases.name: HTTP Lane Activation
actions: Build HTTP queue from DNS positives

assumptions: AWSEM supports per-partition queue depth monitoring and backpressure signals
Neo4j is standalone (not cluster); single-node write path
ClickHouse is on Controller node or accessible with < 10ms network latency
Redis is available on Controller for Bloom filter and cache
Subdomain dataset files are pre-deduplicated at source
ORS has API surface for threshold configuration and Mattermost/webhook alert emission
